title: LinkedIn Future of Work Fund 2026: Global AI Workforce Grant Supporting Nonprofits Preparing Young Adults for the AI Economy
summary: The LinkedIn Future of Work Fund 2026 is a global philanthropic grant initiative designed to help nonprofit organizations prepare young people for a rapidly changing labor market shaped by artificial intelligence. As automation and AI technologies continue transforming industries, LinkedIn is expanding its commitment to economic inclusion by funding organizations that help young adults successfully transition into an AI-powered workforce. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); The 2026 edition builds on LinkedIn s first year of funding nonprofit innovators worldwide and introduces an expanded funding round combining financial grants and in-kind support resources. The program focuses specifically on young adults facing structural barriers to employment and aims to ensure that technological transformation does not widen inequality. Why the Future of Work Fund Matters Artificial intelligence is not just creating new jobs; it is reshaping nearly every existing one. According to LinkedIn projections, approximately 70% of the skills required for jobs will change by 2030. This means traditional career preparation models are becoming outdated. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The challenge is not simply training people to use technology. Instead, the workforce now requires a combination of: AI fluency and digital capability Critical thinking and adaptability Communication and collaboration Problem-solving in uncertain environments ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Many young adults particularly those from underserved backgrounds lack access to training and opportunities that help them adapt to this shift. The Future of Work Fund exists to support organizations solving exactly this problem. What the Fund Supports The fund provides both financial grants and in-kind support to nonprofit organizations delivering programs that expand economic opportunity in an AI-driven labor market. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Key Focus Areas Organizations applying must demonstrate a clear and practical approach to at least one of the following: 1. Preparing Young Adults for AI-Driven Careers Programs that teach AI literacy, digital skills, and workforce readiness for modern job markets. 2. Improving Job Access Initiatives helping career starters connect with employment opportunities using technology and AI-enabled pathways. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); 3. Supporting Workforce Success Training that helps young workers thrive after employment through adaptability, communication, and lifelong learning skills. 4. System-Level Workforce Innovation Projects influencing how education systems, training institutions, or employers integrate AI workforce preparation. What LinkedIn Means by AI Innovation LinkedIn is not only interested in teaching technical coding skills. Instead, the fund prioritizes organizations that combine technological learning with human capabilities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Supported initiatives typically include: AI-fluency training programs Career navigation tools powered by AI Skills-matching platforms Job readiness pathways Human-skill development (communication, collaboration, adaptability) Alternative credential programs Who the Fund Aims to Help The program focuses on young adults entering the workforce who face employment barriers. These may include: Individuals from low-income communities First-generation professionals Learners outside traditional university pathways Underemployed youth Career starters without professional networks ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Typical target age range: 18 24 (varies by country) Eligibility Requirements for Organizations To qualify, applicants must meet all of the following conditions. Organizational Status Must be a legally registered nonprofit, charity, or equivalent not-for-profit organization U.S. organizations must hold 501(c)(3) status Must be able to directly receive grant funding ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Program Focus Organizations must demonstrate: Workforce development programming Clear use of AI innovation OR preparation for AI workforce transition Support for young adults facing economic opportunity barriers Organizations not meeting these requirements are encouraged to use LinkedIn s free nonprofit resources instead. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Funding and Support Structure Selected organizations receive a combination of monetary and strategic assistance. Financial Support Cash grant funding for program implementation and expansion. In-Kind Support Access to LinkedIn expertise Workforce insights and data Platform resources Strategic guidance This hybrid model helps organizations scale impact beyond traditional grants. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Evaluation Criteria Applications are reviewed based on: Demonstrated impact potential Clarity of AI integration Capacity to support underserved young adults Scalability of program model Alignment with economic inclusion goals Application Deadline March 15, 2026 at 12 PM PT Submit your application HERE . Visit the LinkedIn Social Impact website HERE for more details. Click HERE to discover more grant opportunities. title: Apply for SCEG Caribbean Economic Growth Grants 2026 – Mercy Corps Funding Opportunity
summary: The Mercy Corps Strengthening Caribbean Economic Growth (SCEG) Program has launched a Small Grants Challenge aimed at supporting organizations driving sustainable economic development across the Caribbean. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); Through this funding opportunity, up to 12 organization s will receive grants of up to $50,000 USD each to implement impactful, locally driven projects that strengthen livelihoods, businesses, and economic systems in the region. About the SCEG Small Grants Challenge The SCEG initiative is designed to accelerate inclusive economic growth by empowering organisations working to: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Expand employment opportunities across key sectors Strengthen micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs) Improve access to market-driven inputs, services, and business support systems ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); This program prioritizes practical, scalable solutions that can create measurable economic impact within a 12-month project period . Why Apply? This grant offers a powerful opportunity to: Access funding to scale your economic impact Strengthen local business ecosystems and employment Partner with an internationally recognized development organization Deliver measurable results that support long-term economic resilience ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Who Can Apply? This opportunity is open to registered organisations that are based in or operating within the following Caribbean countries: Barbados The Bahamas Grenada Jamaica Saint Kitts and Nevis Organisations may include NGOs, social enterprises, private sector actors, and development-focused institutions that align with the program s goals. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Grant Value and Duration Grant amount: Up to $50,000 USD per organisation Number of awards: Up to 12 grantees Project duration: Maximum of 12 months Key Dates Application Open: February 9, 2026 Application Deadline: February 23, 2026 (11:59 PM AST) How to Apply Interested organisations can submit their applications HERE Opportunity Summary Category Details Program Name Strengthening Caribbean Economic Growth (SCEG) Small Grants Challenge Host Organization Mercy Corps Location Caribbean (Barbados, Bahamas, Grenada, Jamaica, Saint Kitts Nevis) Funding Amount Up to $50,000 USD Deadline February 23, 2026 Project Duration Up to 12 months If your organisation is working to create jobs, support MSMEs, or strengthen economic systems in the Caribbean , this is a strong opportunity to secure funding and scale your impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Apply before the deadline and position your organization at the forefront of economic transformation in the Caribbean. title: Apply Now: International Law Fellows Network 2026 on the Future of the Rule of Law
summary: The global rule of law is under unprecedented strain. Long-standing models of legal cooperation and governance once shaped by post Cold War optimism are now being challenged by political polarisation, populism, and competing global visions of governance. In response, leading institutions are convening the next generation of legal professionals to rethink how the rule of law can be defended and strengthened in the 21st century. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); The International Law Fellows Network 2026: The Future of the Rule of Law in a Fractured World offers a unique opportunity for early- to mid-career lawyers to engage in this urgent conversation and develop practical strategies for safeguarding justice, accountability, and legal integrity worldwide. Why the Rule of Law Matters Now The United States and Europe have historically promoted rule-of-law frameworks through foreign aid, technical assistance, and policy conditionality. However, those approaches are now facing mounting pressure: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); In the United States , political attacks on courts and constitutional structures reveal internal vulnerabilities. Across Europe, democratic institutions are being tested by populism, executive overreach, and disinformation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Globally, rival governance models from Russia and China emphasize sovereignty and centralized control over accountability and individual rights. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); As these trends reshape global governance, rising legal professionals must grapple with how to uphold core principles fairness, equality, transparency, and justice in an increasingly complex world. About the Programme This high-level programme is convened by Salzburg Global Seminar , the International Bar Association (IBA) , and the 21st Century Trust . It brings together early- to mid-career lawyers working in: Major law firms International organizations Public institutions Human rights and advocacy spaces ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Participants will explore how their work in cross-border transactions, compliance, dispute resolution, and human rights law can help defend and adapt the rule of law in a fractured geopolitical landscape. Programme Details Programme Dates: June 11 14, 2026 Location: Schloss Leopoldskron , Salzburg, Austria Application Deadline: April 5, 2026 Fees Funding Fully Funded: Participants selected through IBA member firms or the Salzburg Global Cutler Fellowship community will receive full coverage of programme fees, accommodation, and meals. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); IBA-supported participants may also receive travel funding. General Applicants: Programme cost: $4,250 Employer sponsorship is encouraged. Who Should Apply? Ideal Participants Early- to mid-career lawyers with an interest in international law and global governance ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Professionals engaged in cross-border legal practice or policy work Advocates committed to upholding democratic institutions and human rights Programme Goals The International Law Fellows Network aims to: Strengthen leadership among rising international lawyers Equip participants to defend and adapt rule-of-law frameworks Foster transatlantic collaboration on global legal challenges Promote legal systems grounded in accountability, fairness, and rights Key Questions the Programme Will Explore How can lawyers safeguard the rule of law amid geopolitical fragmentation? ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); What role do legal professionals play in defending democratic institutions? How can global legal frameworks respond to competing governance models? What practical strategies can strengthen transparency, accountability, and justice worldwide? ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); How to Apply Click HERE , To apply. Deadline: April 5, 2026 In a time when legal norms and democratic institutions are increasingly challenged, the need for principled and forward-thinking legal professionals has never been greater. The International Law Fellows Network 2026 offers a powerful platform for rising lawyers to shape the future of global justice and ensure the rule of law remains a cornerstone of free, fair, and stable societies. If you are ready to contribute to this mission, apply today and become part of a global network committed to defending the rule of law. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); For more Fellowship opportunities, click HERE .
